title: Living in Boulder Vlog 2 - 21 -  2017: 40K people in background street view

enrichment.summary: Kyle records a late-night vlog describing a Google Street View experiment where he walked through a 40,000-person crowd visualization. He also discusses his fantasy world 'Boulder Rome,' updates on car troubles (lost service records, broken interior light), and plans for the next day including car sale prep and website work.
enrichment.speaker: kyle

stances.position: Talking publicly where everyone can watch is a good thing, like Donald Trump does
Romans did it wrong building enclosed concrete buildings; open outdoor spaces with gardens are better
It's a bleed-through interdimensional sister city merging Rome and Boulder that influences both worlds

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: I decided to do my 40,000 people simulation again because last time I just hovered above it and swiveled around.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: I wanted to simulate walking along the ground at two meters high in a square of 200 people across.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: I didn't like being on a road in the simulation because I couldn't move around freely.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: In the simulation, yellow represents 10,000, green represents 40,000, and red represents 100.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: I came up with the idea of the King's Court, a fantasy world halfway between Rome and Boulder.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: In Boulder Room, the Kingly Court is outside, unlike Rome's concrete buildings.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: Boulder Room is a public space where everyone can watch interactions, similar to Twitter but in person.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: There's an interdimensional bleed-through between Boulder and Boulder Room.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: I introduced the concept of interdimensional bleed-through into the real world.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: I haven't done a scientific experiment to see how far away people can hear you in the simulation.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: I was up late working until I came to Rome.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: I worked for a couple of hours in Rome before going to my long-south at 12:30.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: I wanted to help clean up for dinner and fix various items like a huddle, belt, and shampoo cover.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: I worked on history books for America and most of the links, but not all.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: I'm now working on UK history books, but I don't think it would be good because they were all part of one thing.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: I still haven't been called back about getting the interior light fixed in my car.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: I lost all of my service records for my car.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: I had a theory that the service records were in a drawer of a piece of furniture in my kitchen.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: If I can't find the service records, I can use my YouTube videos as proof of the motor rebuild.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: I'm pretty high right now.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: Tomorrow, if they call me about the car, I'll pick it up and continue working on it.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: I don't want to do anything to sell the car until I have it.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: I need to pay my bills, but I couldn't get anyone to help last night.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: I need to have my bills sent to the actual branch to manually pay them.
